title: Missing ARIA tree roles and keyboard focusability in registry tree nodes
description: Registry tree nodes (.fabric/rules folder, global.md, dashboard.md, ui-conventions.md) are interactive (hover styles, pointer cursor) but built as plain divs lacking tabindex=0, keyboard activation/navigation listeners, and semantic ARIA roles (role=tree/treeitem, aria-expanded). The entire left registry pane is inaccessible to keyboard/screen-reader users.
fix_direction: Wrap the tree container in role=tree, give each node role=treeitem + tabindex=0, and add keydown handlers for arrows and Enter/Space to manage focus/selection.

title: Color-only and missing ARIA signaling for active registry tree node selection
description: The active tree item (dashboard.md) is styled with a unique color (purple dark / blue light) to signify selection but has no aria-selected/aria-current. Visually-impaired or color-blind users cannot tell which file is currently selected.
fix_direction: Add aria-selected=true (or aria-current=true) to the active node and manage it via JS on click/navigation.

title: Missing accessible state for theme toggle button
description: The theme toggle uses classList.toggle('dark') but does not communicate state to screen readers — static aria-label='Toggle theme', no aria-pressed/aria-live. AT cannot know which theme is applied.
fix_direction: Add a dynamically-updated aria-pressed=true/false, or dynamically update aria-label to state the current/target theme.

title: fabric sync prints raw untranslated store.state with no status markers
description: fabric sync output prints the raw store.state string in English (pending, synced, conflict, offline, aborted) without localization or colored status markers — a localization/readability barrier for non-English (zh-CN) users and no semantic cue for color-blind/low-literacy users.
fix_direction: Map store.state to localizable t(...) strings and paint with theme colors (paint.success for synced, paint.error for conflict/aborted, paint.warn for offline).

title: Redundant visual text and aria-label repetition (nav links, line 86)
description: Sidebar nav links define an aria-label identical to the link text (aria-label='Readiness' with text 'Readiness'), making screen readers read the name twice.
fix_direction: Remove the redundant aria-label when the visible text is already descriptive and identical.

title: Hardcoded English strings in onboard-coverage terminal output
description: The onboard-coverage human-readable renderer uses hardcoded English labels/columns ('Onboard coverage:', 'slot', 'status', 'entries', ...) despite translation support, so CJK users see an untranslated English block.
fix_direction: Wrap column headers, status strings, and helper text in renderHumanReadable with t(...) translator lookups.
